subject: Ecology
title: Amphibians: Wetland Indicators
description: A micro-lesson that makes Amphibians: Wetland Indicators usable.

hook.text: Frogs are the canaries of the wetland world. When they disappear, something is deeply wrong.

buildup.text: Amphibians breathe through their skin, absorbing water—and any pollutants dissolved in it.

discovery.text: Their dual life—aquatic larvae, terrestrial adults—links wetland and upland ecosystems.

twist.text: A fungal disease called chytrid has driven over 90 amphibian species to extinction since the 1970s.

climax.text: Monitoring amphibian populations is one of the best ways to assess wetland health.

punchline.text: If the frogs are silent, the wetland is in trouble.

quiz.question: Why are amphibians good indicators of wetland health?
options: They absorb water and pollutants through their skin
They only live in perfectly clean water
